rows.notes: Page number in top-left corner is crisp, printed numeral '45' clearly legible.
Year '1900.' is centered at top and clearly written in ink; unambiguous.
Page title 'General Acct.' in large script at top-right is clear and unambiguous.
This line is a section label at the left ('Dues composition'). Handwriting is legible though cursive; interpreted as a heading introducing the items below.
Amount '53 17 6' aligns clearly under the pounds/shillings/pence ruling lines at the right. Digits are distinct and values pass currency validation (17s <20, 6d <12).

Text reads 'Balance from General Acct. I' (the trailing 'I' is a Roman numeral for account I). Amount '228 1 6' is aligned in the same columns; three-digit pounds are legible though the hundreds digit is slightly compressed, hence modest uncertainty.

Line reads 'Balance at beginning of Acct.' Amount appears as '8147 17 2' in the right-hand columns. The four-digit pounds '8147' is readable though slightly ornate; shillings and pence digits are clear and within valid ranges.

Final total shown with a stylized pound sign followed by '8450 10 8' in the totals position. Numerals are well-aligned under ruling lines and clearly written; currency columns validate (10s <20, 8d <12).
